author: @Noodleydoo
text: I've been reading FF since 1975, and watched (and loved) most of the MCU movies! We fans were practically knocking each other down trying to get tickets to Infinity War, Winter Soldier, Civil War, Black Panther, etc. We got 3 black Avengers that everyone loved, we got Black Widow, Scarlet Witch, the Dora Milaje, and other great female characters.Everything was great! And then they made the Wasp a generic boss chick type, a martial artist. That is not the Wasp. She was never a physical fighter. It was all downhill from there, sad to say. The people cast in this Fantastic Four movie do not seem at all like the FF I read for years. Johnny should be young, brash, a ladies man, and 20 years old or less. The actor playing him said Johnny should be sensitive to peoples' emotional feelings or some crap like that. Hard pass.

author: @nitrokid
text:
Hmm...I don't know. It's hard to judge a final product that's not here yet through some article in the internet. Truth be told, I'm one of those who experience MCU's superhero fatigue. The last movie I saw was Spider-Man: No Way Home. I haven't seen any MCU episodic series since Hawkeye. Don't even ask me about Star Wars stuff or the recent Daredevil. I just don't care about Disney stuff anymore 😂

BUT! To be honest, the fear regarding this movie, I don't see it. For the lore, that this is set in another universe, I don't care! I read the original comics when I was little and I don't care. I'm a fan of something new, although it'll make a crossover more trickier. And sure, it seems there's some 'woke' stuff or girl boss theme that I don't care, but I don't think it will affect the movie that much. I don't care about the cast, as long the change isn't too radical. And it's okay to write strong women character, if they know how to do it, they should. I'm a fan of strong female characters, having growing up playing Tomb Raider games. Except for the rumored female Silver Surfer, which kinda jarring, but whatever. I don't know if I ever watch MCU films again anyways.
